If at sunset on the full-moon day of Asadha a south-west wind
blows unceasingly tossing up and down in the sea heaps of small
cardamoms, averrhoas and cloves, then the earth shrouded by a
heavy load of scattered bone-pieces of men who have perished by
hunger and thirst, would appear wild and restless like a young
woman who has just lost her husband.
[The style is brilliant and imagery vivid in this verse in which
we come across the ghastly effects of famine, drought and mortality
stalking the land.]
